requirementCueLines: BIS 141 Natural History and Environmental Science

The following degree requirements are required as of Autumn 2024 quarter. Students who entered the Environmental Studies (ENST) major prior to Autumn 2024 have different requirements. Please see your Academic Advisor for questions and academic planning.
B WRIT 133 or B WRIT 134 or ENGL 131 or equivalent (5 credits)
B WRIT 135 or ENGL 141 or equivalent (5 credits)
30 credits of Environmental Studies Core Requirements (ENST:CORE)
BIS 242 Environmental Geography (AUT quarter) OR BIS 243 Intro to Environmental Issues (WIN quarter) (5 credits)
B GIS 342 Geographic Information Systems (5 credits)
BIS 356 Ethics and the Environment (5 credits) (AUT or SPR quarter) OR BIS 386 Climate Change Adaptation (5 credits) (WIN or SPR quarter)
BES 301 Science Methods & Practice OR BST 301 Scientific Writing (5 Credits)
Residency Requirement: 30 credits must be completed in residency at UW Bothell.

Interdisciplinary Practices & Reflection (IPR): The IPR requirement can overlap with the 70 credits of major coursework, or it can be completed through elective credits. Please see the IPR webpage for course options.

Upper Division Credit Policy: A maximum of 35 credits earned in 100- and 200-level courses may apply toward the major. Remaining credits must be earned in upper division courses (300-level and above).
BEARTH 310 Fundamentals of Weather and Climate
